target.caption: This instrumental piece is a minimalist electronic‑ambient work that blends subtle glitch textures with a steady, mid‑tempo pulse. It features sparse synth pads, delicate arpeggiated figures, and occasional metallic percussive clicks, creating a calm yet slightly unsettling atmosphere. The production emphasizes spaciousness through generous reverb and delay, allowing each element to breathe within a wide stereo field while maintaining a clean, high‑fidelity mix. No vocal lines are present, so there are no lyrical themes or melodic hooks to describe. Overall, the track evokes a contemplative, introspective mood suitable for background listening or focused study environments.
target.title: Slow Down

target.caption:
This piece is an experimental electronic instrumental that blends glitch‑hop textures with abstract noise aesthetics. It runs at approximately 81 BPM (no fixed time signature) and is centered in C major according to the supplied metadata.
Instrumentation & production: The arrangement relies on heavily processed synthesizers generating distorted drones, metallic percussive hits, and granular sound‑design elements. A deep sub‑bass underpins the low end while sparse, high‑frequency clicks punctuate the surface. The mix emphasizes extreme stereo panning and spatial movement, creating a disorienting sense of depth. Compression is aggressive, yielding consistently loud dynamics, and extensive distortion, reverb, and delay shape every element into a harsh yet immersive wall of sound.
Vocals: None – the track contains no vocal performance.
Lyrical themes: Not applicable.
Structure & harmonic flow: The composition unfolds as a continuous loop rather than conventional verse‑chorus form. Harmonic material cycles through a sequence that includes “N” (silence), Dm7♭5/C → Am → Gmaj7 → F♯aug → Emaj7 → Bbmaj7 → Abmaj7 → E♭dim → C6 → N. This progression moves from minor
target.title: DreamTOXIC

target.caption:
This instrumental piece is a dark‑ambient / industrial electronic composition that blends brooding synth textures with harsh mechanical percussion. It runs at ≈ 76.9 BPM (no fixed time signature) and is rooted in C major according to the supplied key information.
The arrangement relies on deep, sustained synthesizer pads that create an ominous atmosphere, layered over gritty, metallic percussive hits that function as rhythmic punctuation rather than a conventional drum groove. The chord progression cycles through simple tonal centers—C → Fmaj7 → G → Am → Em—with occasional “N” (silence) moments that heighten tension before returning to the tonic. Production emphasizes heavy distortion, cavernous reverb, and wide stereo imaging, giving the mix a dense, immersive feel while keeping dynamics relatively flat but intensifying toward the climax.
There are no vocal parts; the track is purely instrumental.
Lyrically there is nothing to report, so thematic focus rests entirely on the sonic narrative: the evolving layers evoke feelings of dread, isolation, and relentless forward motion, fitting within the broader context of early‑2020s experimental electronic works that merge ambient ambience with industrial aggression.
Overall, the track delivers a bleak yet compelling soundscape that
target.title: The Crow Song

target.caption: This piece is an Ambient / Drone composition that blends ethereal synth pads with deep sub‑bass drones, creating a spacious, meditative soundscape. The texture is built from slowly evolving analog‑style synths and low‑frequency rumblings, layered with subtle high‑frequency shimmering tones that give the track its luminous quality. No percussion or rhythmic elements appear, allowing the listener to focus on the continuous harmonic flow. Production emphasizes wide stereo imaging, gentle compression, and generous reverb/delay to enhance depth and immersion. The overall mood is calm, introspective, and slightly mysterious, suitable for relaxation, meditation, or background ambience. It could serve as a backdrop for visual media seeking a serene, otherworldly atmosphere.
target.title: Sparkles from the Outside

source.caption: This instrumental piece is a gentle Classical piano solo that blends Romantic-era lyricism with modern high‑fidelity recording techniques. The sole instrument is a grand piano played softly with expressive dynamics, delivering a flowing melody over rich, resonant chords. Production emphasizes natural acoustic ambience, wide stereo imaging, and subtle reverb that lets every nuance—from delicate arpeggios to warm bass notes—ring clearly without compression artifacts. No vocals appear, so the focus remains entirely on the piano’s timbre and phrasing. The overall mood is calm, reflective, and slightly nostalgic, evoking quiet contemplation suitable for study, relaxation, or background listening.
source.title: Classical

target.caption: This instrumental piece is a contemporary classical work that blends minimalist piano textures with subtle electronic ambience. The composition features a solo grand piano playing arpeggiated chords and melodic fragments over sustained synth pads, creating a calm yet emotionally resonant atmosphere. Production emphasizes high-fidelity recording, wide stereo imaging, and gentle compression that lets the piano’s natural resonance sit alongside the warm, spacious pad layers without competing for space. No vocals appear, so the focus remains entirely on the evolving harmonic landscape. Structurally, the track unfolds as a continuous development: an opening piano motif introduces the tonal center, followed by gradual layering of additional harmonies and rhythmic pulses from the synths, leading to a middle section where tension builds through denser chord voicings before gently receding back toward the initial material for a reflective finale. Overall, the mood is introspective and serene, evoking quiet contemplation suitable for study, meditation, or cinematic underscoring.
target.title: Avenew

source.caption:
This piece is a high‑energy instrumental that blends classic rockabilly swing with big‑band jazz sensibilities, creating a retro yet lively stylistic hybrid. The arrangement centers on a bright brass section (trumpets, trombones) delivering punchy melodic lines, supported by a walking upright bass, crisp ride cymbal patterns, and occasional piano comping that adds harmonic color. Production is clean and vintage‑inspired: drums sit front‑and‑center with tight compression, brass is spread wide across the stereo field, and the overall mix emphasizes clarity and separation so each instrument cuts through without clutter.
Vocals consist solely of brief spoken interjections from male voices. One voice delivers a formal address—“You’re the gentleman that vexed him”—with a slightly nasal timbre and natural room reverb, while another responds promptly with “Yes, sir.” These snippets function as narrative punctuation rather than lyrical verses, adding a conversational, theatrical touch typical of early‑20th‑century vaudeville performances.
Lyrically there is no sung material; the only vocal content is the short dialogue above, which reinforces the playful, nostalgic atmosphere.
Structurally, the track opens with a brisk drum intro that establishes the swing groove, followed
source.title: Jazz Begin

target.caption:
This piece is a dark‑toned French hip‑hop/trap instrumental that blends gritty street rap rhythms with moody electronic textures. It sits firmly in the trap sub‑genre while borrowing atmospheric synth pads from contemporary French urban music, creating a stylistic hybrid of hard‑hitting beats and brooding ambience.

The duration of the piece is 30.05 seconds.
Tempo & Meter – The track runs at ≈ 96.8 BPM in 4/4 time, giving it a laid‑back yet driving pulse that underpins the lyrical flow.
Key & Harmony – Centered on A minor, the harmonic backdrop cycles through simple minor‑key progressions such as Am → Dm7♭5/C → Fmaj7 → C♯dim, punctuated by occasional major chords (A major, Bb) and dominant sevenths (E7/G#). This mixture of diatonic minor movement and chromatic color adds tension before resolving back to the tonic, reinforcing the track’s introspective vibe.
Instrumentation & Production – The beat is built around punchy electronic drums: crisp hi‑hats, rapid snare rolls, and a deep, resonant 808 kick
target.title: Petite Secrétaire

target.caption: This piece is a dark‑ambient / industrial‑drone composition that blends brooding atmospheric textures with harsh mechanical percussion. It unfolds as a slow‑moving soundscape built from deep sub‑bass drones, metallic clangs, grinding metal scrapes, and sparse percussive hits that punctuate the evolving layers. The production emphasizes spacious reverb and wide stereo imaging, allowing each element—from low rumbling bass to high‑frequency hiss—to occupy its own depth while maintaining a cohesive, oppressive ambience. No vocal lines are present, so there are no timbral or lyrical contributions to describe. Structurally, the track progresses through gradual layering: an opening swell of drone gives way to intermittent metallic impacts, then recedes into quieter passages before building again toward a final, sustained climax where all elements converge. Overall, the mood is ominous, unsettling, and cinematic, evoking desolate industrial wastelands and reinforcing themes of decay and isolation within a minimalist yet richly textured sonic environment.
target.title: Мёртвый Груз - 02 - Мы подземные твари

target.caption: This is a melancholic French alternative‑rock piece that blends indie‑rock sensibilities with subtle electronic textures. The arrangement centers on clean electric guitars playing arpeggiated chords, warm synth pads filling out the harmonic space, a steady bass line anchoring the low end, and crisp acoustic drums providing a relaxed yet propulsive rhythm. Production is polished and high‑fidelity: guitars sit wide in the stereo field, synths add depth without crowding the mix, and the overall balance emphasizes clarity and spaciousness while keeping volume levels consistent throughout. Vocals are delivered by a male baritone singing in French; his timbre is smooth and slightly breathy, conveying introspection and emotional weight. The performance is melodic and expressive, treated with moderate reverb and delay plus light compression to preserve intimacy. Lyrically, the song explores themes of disorientation and inner reflection, illustrated by lines such as “Des jours entiers sans savoir où j’en suis chaque nuit qui passe à ressasser mes idées noires” (“Days spent without knowing where I am, every night passing by, ruminating on my dark ideas”). These verses reinforce the reflective atmosphere created by the instrumentation. Structurally, the composition follows a conventional verse‑chorus form with instrumental breaks
target.title: Mes Idées Noires

source.caption:
This piece is an instrumental rock‑infused electronic track that blends gritty guitar riffs with punchy synth bass and crisp drum programming, creating a high‑energy, forward‑driving feel. The arrangement leans on distorted electric guitars playing tight power chords, layered over bright, side‑stereo synths that add melodic hooks and atmospheric pads. Production is polished yet retains a raw edge: drums sit front‑and‑center with tight compression, guitars are panned wide for width, and the mix emphasizes clarity while preserving a slightly aggressive tone. No vocal lines appear, so the focus remains entirely on the instrumental interplay.
The composition follows a straightforward verse‑chorus structure typical of modern alternative rock, punctuated by brief instrumental breaks where the guitars take the lead before returning to the hook. Dynamic shifts are subtle but effective—the verses stay relatively restrained, building tension through rhythmic syncopation, then explode into full‑band choruses that reinforce the driving momentum.
Overall, the track conveys an upbeat, confident mood suited to energetic settings such as live shows or workout playlists, reflecting contemporary trends where organic rock instrumentation meets electronic polish.
source.title: Rubicon of Gits.

target.caption: This piece is a raw spoken‑word/industrial‑ambient vignette that blends stark narrative delivery with gritty electronic textures. It features a deep male voice speaking English in a calm yet urgent tone over sparse industrial percussion (metallic hits, low thuds) and a subtle sub‑bass drone, creating a tense, dystopian atmosphere reminiscent of underground protest recordings or post‑apocalyptic soundscapes. The duration of the piece is 30.05 seconds.
target.title: LONDON ft PL-Ease and Teeth Collider

target.caption: This piece is a dark‑ambient / industrial spoken‑word composition that blends brooding atmospheric textures with harsh mechanical percussion. It unfolds as a slow, deliberate soundscape built around deep synth drones, metallic clangs, low rumbling bass tones, and sparse electronic clicks that punctuate the silence. The production emphasizes spacious reverb and subtle delay on every element, creating an immersive stereo field where the listener feels enveloped by oppressive ambience rather than driven by rhythm. Vocals consist solely of heavily processed male spoken word samples delivered in English. These fragments are filtered through distortion, pitch shifting, granular synthesis, and long reverbs/delays, rendering them almost robotic and disembodied. Their timbre is thin yet resonant, functioning more as narrative punctuation than melodic singing. Lyrical themes revolve around existential questioning and confinement, repeatedly asking “Is it about life or death?” and echoing lines such as “Cause I won’t tell you lies free,” “Cause I won’t let you lie free,” and “Cause we won’t let me die.” Other excerpts include “It’s all within your mind” and “Your fucking free but if you say another line like you burn your house,” underscoring a sense of inner turmoil and fatalistic reflection. Struct
target.title: I Could Burn Your House

target.caption: This is a high‑energy punk rock track that leans toward garage‑punk aggression, driven by fast, distorted electric guitars playing tight power chords, a punchy bass line, and rapid, hard‑hitting drums that keep the momentum relentless. The raw, lo‑fi production captures a gritty live feel, while the vocal delivery is shouted male singing with a strained, urgent timbre typical of classic punk frontmen. Lyrically, the song channels themes of deception and betrayal, opening with lines such as “Someone once told me he would have put my trust” and “Someone wants me through his magic,” underscoring a sense of mistrust and personal conflict. Structurally, the piece follows a straightforward verse‑chorus format punctuated by brief instrumental breaks that let the guitars riff freely before launching back into the next vocal section, maintaining constant intensity from start to finish. Overall, the track exudes a rebellious, confrontational mood rooted in early‑90s underground punk aesthetics, delivering an unpolished yet powerful sonic assault.
target.title: Chains of a martyr

source.caption: This track is a high‑energy punk rock piece that leans into classic garage‑rock aggression while incorporating post‑punk angularity. It drives forward with fast‑paced electric guitars drenched in distortion, punchy bass lines, and relentless drumming that propels the song’s urgent momentum. The raw vocal delivery sits front‑and‑center, delivering shouted, slightly gritty verses that match the rebellious spirit typical of late‑70s/early‑80s UK punk scenes. Lyrically, the song channels defiant self‑assertion and cryptic commentary on observation and consequence, as heard in lines like “Refused thought
source.title: The 15th

target.caption: This post‑punk track features jangly guitars layered over steady bass and drums, creating a raw yet melodic indie rock atmosphere typical of early 80s British alternative music. The rhythm is mid‑tempo with a driving beat that supports introspective male vocals delivered in English with a detached tone. Lyrically, the song paints surreal images such as “A total eclipse arrives now and Niagara Falls,” hints at uncertainty with lines like “No loss of life yet… I never know which version I’m going to be,” and adds cryptic reflections (“I told her clips arrive now and no further call”). source.caption: This piece is a minimalist electronic instrumental that blends lo‑fi synth textures with subtle glitch elements, creating a calm yet slightly unsettling atmosphere. The arrangement centers on soft, sustained synthesizer pads and delicate arpeggiated figures, supported by sparse percussion clicks and low‑frequency drones that give the track a spacious, immersive feel. Production emphasizes gentle compression and wide stereo imaging, allowing each layer to breathe while maintaining a cohesive, introspective vibe. No vocal lines appear, so the focus remains entirely on the evolving harmonic landscape. Overall, the composition evokes a reflective, almost meditative mood suitable for background listening or focused study sessions.
source.title: Two Time

target.caption: This piece is an experimental electronic‑ambient work that blends glitch‑infused textures with abstract sound design. It moves at a slow, free‑flowing pace (tempo not specified) and lacks a fixed time signature, creating a spacious, contemplative atmosphere. The instrumentation centers on heavily processed synthesizers, granular synth layers, and subtle field recordings that generate evolving drones and fragmented melodic fragments. Production emphasizes extreme dynamic contrast—from whisper‑soft whispers to sudden, jarring bursts—and extensive use of reverb, delay, distortion, and pitch shifting to sculpt an unsettling yet immersive sonic environment. No vocal elements are present, so there
target.title: Transit

target.caption: This instrumental piece is a dark‑ambient composition that blends cinematic orchestration with subtle electronic textures, creating an epic yet melancholic atmosphere. The arrangement features lush string sections, resonant piano chords, deep synth pads, and sparse metallic percussion, all rendered with high‑fidelity, spacious mixing that emphasizes wide stereo imaging and gentle dynamic shifts. No vocal lines appear, allowing the instrumentation alone to convey its somber narrative tone. The overall feel evokes introspection and grandeur, suitable for film scoring or immersive listening experiences.
target.title: Grace
